svg.title: Fig. 3 — Radar charts: multi-dimensional similarity analysis (Ayaan & Ng, 2025)
svg.desc: Four radar charts comparing student and reference answers across Jaccard, Inverted Edit, Cosine, Normal Words, Semantic, and Final score metrics. Cases: (a) high similarity, (b) moderate similarity, (c) low similarity — hard zero, (d) partial similarity.
svg.text: Fig. 3 — Radar charts: multi-dimensional similarity analysis
Comparison of Students Answers and References
Ceiling rounding awards full marks even though branch was partial.
Comparison of Students Answers and References
Student shows partial understanding with notable metric divergence.
Comparison of Students Answers and References
Stf=0.191 < 0.20 → hard zero branch fires. F=0. All other scores ignored.
NW=1.3 (verbose) but semantic mismatch means zero marks awarded.
Comparison of Students Answers and References
NW=1.8 capped. Extended answer with incomplete semantic match.
Blue polygon = reference answer (max value 1.0 on all axes) · Pink polygon = student answer · Grid rings at 0.2 intervals
NW values > 1.0 (cases c, d) are capped to 1.0 for display · Hard zero fires when Stf < 0.20 regardless of other metrics
